steps.title: Track the causal relationship
steps.content: The sentence compares the lion's top running speed with the swift's flying speed, then explains why the two speeds differ. The words after the blank identify the cause: flight-related features do less to limit top speed than running-related features do. So the blank must describe the speed difference as the result of that physical fact, not as a goal, denial, or explanation of it.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong relationships
steps.content: Choice A - a repudiation. This means a rejection or denial, but the speed difference does not reject the fact about physical features. Choice B - an explanation. This reverses the logic, because the physical features explain the speed difference, not the other way around. Choice C - an objective. This would make the speed difference a goal, which does not fit. Choice D - a consequence. This means a result or effect, matching the cause-and-effect relationship in the sentence. The answer is D.
